title: Observation
description: A system-metric assertion with evidence. One per line in JSONL files. v0.2 — adds uncertainty (lower/upper/CI), assumptions[], normalized_value, scale_level (docs/framework/03 §2, §28).

normalized_value.description: Optional. Value after normalization per the metric's `normalization.strategy`. Stored alongside the raw `value` so analysis can choose either; never replaces the raw value. From docs/framework/03 §2 (`MetricObservation` shape).

uncertainty.description: Optional quantitative uncertainty bounds. Distinct from `confidence`, which scores how trustworthy the source/derivation is. From docs/framework/03 §2.

assumptions.description: Interpretive assumptions used to derive the value (e.g. "edges represent active dependencies", "weights normalized to [0,1]"). From docs/framework/03 §2.

scale_level.description: What level of description this observation describes. Should be consistent with the metric's `scale_level` if both are set; observations may legitimately drill in (e.g. a macro-level metric reported at meso scope for a specific subsystem).

review_state.description: Lifecycle state of this observation. 'proposed' is the cautious entry state (agent is unsure, wants human eyes). 'auto-validated' is the default agent-written state: the agent judges the record acceptable for downstream use but has not received explicit human sign-off. 'validated' requires human or review-records agent sign-off. 'superseded' means replaced by a later observation. 'rejected' means disqualified with rationale.
